creator: peter.jeremy@ALCATEL-LUCENT.COM
text:
(I realise this PR was closed a year ago but I've just run into the
identical problem with rt 3.8.6 and this PR was Google's only hit and
pav@'s advice was not helpful - the mechanism by which a script
executing for ~12 seconds was triggering an 8 hour timeout was not
obvious.  This summary will hopefully help the next person).

In my case, the problem was the presence of a var/data/gpg directory
that was triggering GnuPG::Interface - which was looking for /bin/gpg
and /usr/bin/gpg (only - not /usr/local/bin/gpg - where gpg is found)
in a subprocess.  Without gpg, the sub-process uses 'die' to report an
error and exit.  Unfortunately, somewhere along the line, 'die' has
been overloaded to report an error (which is swallowed somewhere and
only visible via ktrace/truss) and then recover (or maybe do an
excessive amount of cleanup before exiting).  The child then
(incorrectly) accesses and closes the MySQL socket before exiting.
When the parent later attempts to access MySQL, it receives a "not
connected" error which is the one reported.

Overall, there are a number of bugs, though pav@ is correct that none
appear to be FreeBSD-specific:
1) The search path for gpg is incorrect.
2) Errors reported by the forked should-become-gpg process are never
   returned to the user.
3) Stray file descriptors are not closed prior to the attempted exec
   (though the code does appear to expect close-on-exec).
4) 'die' is overloaded, leading to the MySQL connection inherited from
   the parent being closed.

-- 
Peter Jeremy
